skill.name: security-requirement-extraction
skill.description: Derive security requirements from threat models and business context. Use when translating threats into actionable requirements, creating security user stories, or building security test cases.
skill.summary: Derive security requirements from threat models and business context. Use when translating threats i...
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and dismissed as false positives. The command detections are markdown code fences, the network detections are documentation links, and the sensitive, C2, crypto, and reconnaissance detections are security-domain terminology in examples.

content.value_statement: Threat models often stop at risks without clear implementation work. This skill converts threats, compliance needs, and business context into testable security requirements.
actual_capabilities: Converts STRIDE-style threats into functional security requirements.
Creates acceptance criteria for authentication, authorization, logging, and data protection.
Generates security user stories from requirement objects.
Maps requirements to common compliance frameworks.
Builds traceability matrices between threats, controls, and requirements.
limitations: It provides templates and guidance, not a full requirements management system.
Compliance mappings are examples and need review against current regulations.
Generated requirements depend on the quality of the supplied threat model.
It does not execute scanners or validate an implementation.

use_cases.title: Turn threat models into backlog items
use_cases.description: Convert identified threats into security stories with acceptance criteria and test cases.

use_cases.title: Prepare compliance traceability
use_cases.description: Map security requirements to PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, OWASP, or related controls.

use_cases.title: Define secure development requirements
use_cases.description: Create implementation-ready requirements for authentication, authorization, logging, and data protection.

prompt_templates.title: Create basic requirements
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create security requirements for this feature: [feature]. Include requirement titles, rationale, and acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Convert threats to stories
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this threat model: [threat model]. Convert each threat into security user stories with priorities, acceptance criteria, and test cases.

prompt_templates.title: Build compliance mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Map these security requirements to [framework]. Identify covered controls, weak coverage, and missing requirement areas.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a traceability matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a traceability matrix from these threats, requirements, controls, and test cases. Highlight gaps and conflicting priorities.

output_examples.input: A login service has spoofing and brute-force threats.
output_examples.output: The skill produces authentication requirements, rate limiting criteria, logging expectations, and negative test cases for invalid credentials.

output_examples.input: A payment workflow must align with PCI DSS.
output_examples.output: The skill maps data protection, authentication, authorization, and audit logging requirements to relevant control areas.

output_examples.input: A team needs security backlog stories for an API release.
output_examples.output: The skill creates prioritized user stories with definitions of done, acceptance criteria, and traceability to source threats.

best_practices: Start with specific threats, affected assets, and business impact.
Make every requirement testable with clear acceptance criteria.
Review compliance mappings with the responsible governance owner.
anti_patterns: Do not accept vague requirements such as "make it secure".
Do not use generated mappings as final legal or compliance advice.
Do not skip stakeholder review for high-risk requirements.

faq.question: What inputs work best with this skill?
faq.answer: Threat models, architecture notes, compliance targets, and feature descriptions work best.

faq.question: Can it create security user stories?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for user stories, acceptance criteria, definitions of done, and test cases.

faq.question: Does it support compliance mapping?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes example mappings for PCI DSS, HIPAA, GDPR, OWASP, and related frameworks.

faq.question: Does it replace a security architect?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure requirements, but human review is still required for design decisions.

faq.question: Can it validate an implemented system?
faq.answer: No. It creates requirements and test ideas, but it does not run security tests.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
